2605.05863.title: SOPE: Stabilizing Off-Policy Evaluation for Online RL with Prior Data
2605.05863.paper_summary: Incorporating prior data into online reinforcement learning accelerates training but typically forces a difficult trade-off between high computational costs and long, multi-stage training pipelines. While fixed-length stabilization phases are significantly more computationally efficient than static update schedules, they require task-dependent manual tuning, risking either the waste of prior knowledge or severe overfitting. To address this, we propose SOPE, an algorithm that uses an actor-aligned Off-Policy Policy Evaluation (OPE) signal as an automated early-stopping mechanism to dynamically control the length of offline training phases. By evaluating the critic on a held-out validation split under the current policy's action distribution, SOPE halts gradient updates exactly when out-of-distribution benefits saturate, eliminating the need for manual schedule tuning. Evaluated on 25 continuous control tasks from the Minari benchmark suite, SOPE improves baseline performance by up to 45.6% while reducing the required TFLOPs by up to 22x, thus balancing the tradeoff between sample and computational efficiency. These findings demonstrate that adaptive, evaluation-driven update schedules are more effective than relying on static, exhaustive update schedules.
2605.05863.paper_summary_zh: 將先前的數據納入在線增強學習可以加速訓練，但通常會迫使在高計算成本和長期、多階段訓練流程之間做出困難的權衡。雖然固定長度的穩定階段在計算效率上顯著高於靜態更新計劃，但它們需要依賴任務的手動調整，這可能導致先前知識的浪費或嚴重的過擬合。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了SOPE，一種使用與演員對齊的離線策略評估（OPE）信號作為自動早停機制的算法，以動態控制離線訓練階段的長度。通過在當前策略的行動分佈下對保留的驗證數據集評估評論者，SOPE在出現分佈外的效益飽和時精確停止梯度更新，消除了手動調整計劃的需要。在Minari基準套件中的25個連續控制任務上進行評估，SOPE將基準性能提高了最高45.6%，同時將所需的TFLOPs減少了最高22倍，從而平衡了樣本和計算效率之間的權衡。這些發現表明，基於評估驅動的自適應更新計劃比依賴靜態的、全面的更新計劃更有效。
2605.05863.author: Carlo Romeo et.al.
2605.05863.authors: Carlo Romeo, Girolamo Macaluso, Alessandro Sestini, Andrew D. Bagdanov
